name: nvidia-cuda-gdb
title: nvidia-cuda-gdb
description:
NVIDIA CUDA Debugger (GDB)
The Compute Unified Device Architecture (CUDA) enables NVIDIA
graphics processing units (GPUs) to be used for massively parallel
general purpose computation.
.
This package contains the cuda-gdb debugger.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for cuda-gcore.
arguments.raw: -gcore  Option present in package usage line for cuda-gcore.

commands.name: cuda-gdb
commands.description: This is the GNU debugger with CUDA support.  Usage:

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cuda-gcore because NVIDIA CUDA Debugger (GDB).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cuda-gcore as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cuda-gcore with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cuda-gcore --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
